bullets.text: Lebanon has begun peace talks with Israel, but the Lebanese government appears to have limited influence over the group, as reported by the BBC.

The wife of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, Begoña Gómez, has been charged with corruption following a two-year investigation into allegations she used her relationship to advance her private career.
A treaty to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius has become
A man wanted by NSW Police died in hospital from injuries sustained after being detained by two men near Murwillumbah.
Colombian officials have approved a plan to cull dozens of hippos, years after drug lord Pablo Escobar introduced the first ones to the country.
A man has been charged with the murder of his pregnant ex-partner, Hayley McClenahan-Ernst, nine years after her suspicious death in Sydney's west.
OpenAI's investors are questioning the company's $852bn valuation as CEO Sam Altman shifts the AI company's strategy, while China's export of high-tech goods is set to impact global industries.
The war in Iran has led to a significant slowdown in China's export growth and a surge in imports, impacting global energy supply and causing prolonged volatility in energy markets.
The conflict in the Middle East is causing shifts in financial markets, with Hong Kong dollar bond sales booming due to haven flows and Japan's 20-year bond auction seeing strong demand.
